stories.original_headline: Scientists compared dinosaurs to mammals for decades but missed this key difference
stories.original_summary: Baby dinosaurs weren’t coddled like lion cubs or elephant calves—they were more like prehistoric latchkey kids. New research suggests that young dinosaurs quickly struck out on their own, forming kid-only groups and surviving without much parental help, while their massive parents lived entirely dif
stories.fantasy_headline: Young Thunder-Lizards Left Parents Early, Formed Their Own Packs!
stories.fantasy_story: Wizards have uncovered a surprising truth about ancient thunder-lizards. Unlike many creatures today, young thunder-lizards weren't doted on by their massive parents. Instead, they quickly banded together in their own groups, learning to survive without much help. Their parents lived entirely different lives.

stories.original_headline: Ireland’s Old Irish Goat has survived 3,000 years
stories.original_summary: The Old Irish Goat isn’t just part of folklore — it’s genetically linked to goats that lived in Ireland 3,000 years ago. Scientists analyzed ancient remains and discovered that today’s rare breed shares its strongest DNA ties with Late Bronze Age animals. The finding suggests an unbroken Irish linea
stories.fantasy_headline: Ancient Goats of the Emerald Isles Endure Through Three Millennia!
stories.fantasy_story: Lorekeepers of the Emerald Isles have confirmed a marvel! The Old Irish Goat, long a creature of legend, is truly kin to beasts from three thousand years past. Scholars studying ancient remains found a direct bloodline to Late Bronze Age animals. This rare breed holds an unbroken lineage, a testament to ancient magic.
